patch_reason: Clarify continuous worker handoff for follow-on mtasks in multi-worker mode.
objective: Implement Phase-1 worker mtask autopilot so the worker can continuously run the loop: git pull -> detect next assigned approved_to_execute mtask -> execute bounded script -> write result -> git add/commit/push, without manual step-by-step intervention.
required_validation: Script must enforce single-instance lock (flock or lock file guard)
Script must perform git pull --ff-only before each cycle
Script must poll git/task queue every 180 seconds when idle
Script must immediately rescan for the next mtask after each completed task (no idle wait between back-to-back tasks)
Script must identify worker by explicit worker_id and only claim tasks with assigned_to equal to that worker_id
Script must select only tasks assigned_to=ubuntu-worker-01 and status=approved_to_execute
Script must skip tasks that already have a result file
Script must execute only declared executor_script from task, if present
Script must write structured failure result on execution or validation failure
Script must git add/commit/push result artifacts after each processed task
After TASK-0015 result push, autopilot process remains active and ready to claim the next assigned task without manual restart
Provide a dry-run mode showing candidate task selection and no writes
Run one real test cycle against a synthetic local test mtask and provide evidence
execution_instructions: Start with cd ~/mide-pilot && git pull --ff-only origin main
Implement pilot_v1/scripts/worker_mtask_autopilot.sh with strict bash options: set -euo pipefail
Use a lock guard to prevent concurrent runs

Find next task deterministically by task_id order from pilot_v1/tasks/*.json where assigned_to=ubuntu-worker-01 and status=approved_to_execute and no corresponding results/TASK-xxxx.result.json exists
Support worker identity via --worker-id or WORKER_ID environment variable
When idle, sleep exactly 180 seconds by default (override allowed via --poll-seconds)
After each task completion, immediately run another selection pass before any sleep
If task has executor_script, run it from repo root; if missing, write failed result explaining missing executor_script
Always capture stdout/stderr excerpts into result JSON
Commit format for autopilot output: worker: autopilot result <TASK-ID>
Create a short operator note in pilot_v1/state/worker_autopilot_status.json including last_run_utc and last_task_processed
After pushing TASK-0015.result.json, keep autopilot running so it can immediately claim TASK-0016 if present and assigned
Write pilot_v1/results/TASK-0015.result.json with implementation evidence and test-cycle output
git add, git commit -m 'worker: TASK-0015 result -- autopilot loop implemented', git push
